title: Duplicate ISBN raises unhandled 500
evidence: app.py:36 declares `isbn TEXT UNIQUE`; app.py:90 INSERT and app.py:170 UPDATE do not catch sqlite3.IntegrityError, so a duplicate isbn returns a 500 stack trace instead of a validated 4xx
suggestion: Wrap the INSERT/UPDATE in try/except sqlite3.IntegrityError and return 409/400 JSON

title: Unreachable 400 branch in create_book
evidence: app.py:66-67 `if not data: return 400` is dead code — request.get_json() at app.py:64 raises 415 on a non-JSON/empty body, which test_app.py:71-74 asserts (expects 415, not 400)
suggestion: Use request.get_json(silent=True) if a JSON 400 is intended, or drop the branch

title: debug=True and bind to 0.0.0.0
evidence: app.py:202 app.run(debug=True, host="0.0.0.0", port=5000) exposes the Werkzeug debugger on all interfaces
suggestion: Disable debug and bind to 127.0.0.1 for non-dev use
